title: The craziest part of Musk v. Altman happened while the jury was out of the room
summary: During Elon Musk's testimony in his trial against OpenAI, an unusual courtroom moment occurred that may have had legal implications for Musk's case.
content:
Okay, I am not a lawyer so I only understood about half of what just happened. But I am fairly sure, given the context, that Elon Musk's lawyers may have just fucked up big.
Jared "James Brickhouse" Birchall, Musk's finance guy and all-around fixer, took the stand after Musk today. Most of his testimony was dull and seemed to exist primarily to get some documents read into the record, which sucks but is a normal part of sitting through trials. But at the very end of his boring testimony something interesting happened. I believe we all got a surprise, something that rarely happens in courtrooms.
The lawyer conducting his direct examination  …

title: Roblox’s daily users continue to drop as age checks slow growth
summary: Roblox's daily active users fell to 132 million in Q1 2026, down from 144 million a year earlier, as the platform's age verification rollout slowed new user acquisition despite revenue growing to $1.4 billion.
content:
Roblox's daily active users continued to slip last quarter due in part to its rollout of age checks on its platform. According to its latest earnings report, Roblox currently has 132 million daily active users globally, down from 144 million at the end of last year, which was a drop from 152 million in Q3 2025. In the US and Canada, the number of active users dropped by one million from the previous quarter, while Roblox's revenue still grew to $1.4 billion.
Roblox says Q1 growth was "tempered by greater-than-expected headwinds" due to the rollout of its age-check features, which "slowed new user acquisition." Russia's December 2025 ban on  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: Congress keeps kicking surveillance reform down the road
summary: Congress reauthorized Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act for only 45 days, deferring broader reform negotiations on the controversial wiretapping authority despite ongoing debate.
content:
Congress has reauthorized Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act - but only for another 45 days. The extension is meant to give legislators more time to negotiate reforms to the controversial wiretapping bill. If the past few weeks are any indication of how future debates will go, however, we're in for a bumpy ride.
The House renewed Section 702 with minor reforms on Wednesday evening. The bill didn't include the hotly debated warrant requirement, but it did feature a provision prohibiting the Federal Reserve from issuing Central Bank Digital Currencies, which Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-SD) described as a nonstar …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: Apple’s iPhone revenue jumps to $57 billion despite chip shortages
summary: Apple's iPhone revenue increased 22 percent to $57 billion in Q2 2026 despite supply chain constraints, with CEO Tim Cook citing unprecedented demand while warning of potential supply issues for other products.
content:
Apple's iPhone revenue jumped 22 percent to $57 billion over the past few months, despite supply chain issues impacting the device's processor. In an interview with Reuters, Apple CEO Tim Cook said iPhone "demand was off the charts," but there's "a little less flexibility at the moment for getting more parts." Cook added during an earnings call that supply constraints could come for Mac, too, "given the continued high levels of demand that we're seeing."
Apple earned $111.2 billion in revenue during the second quarter of 2026, its "best March quarter ever," according to Cook. This latest earnings report comes a little over a week after the  …

title: Rivian’s revenue is up as R2 production kicks into gear
summary: Rivian reported Q1 2026 earnings showing a 20 percent year-over-year increase in vehicle sales to 10,365 units and a 30 percent production increase, as the company begins manufacturing its crucial R2 electric vehicle.
content:
Rivian reported its first quarter earnings of 2026, providing us a closer look at the company's financial health as it kicks off production for the crucial R2 electric vehicle. 
We've already got Rivian's production and delivery statement from the first three months of the year. The company sold 10,365 vehicles in Q1, representing 20 percent increase year over year. And it produced 10,236 vehicles at its factory in Normal, Illinois, which represents a 30 percent increase compared to the same period last year. The company also reaffirmed its prediction that it will sell 62,000-67,000 vehicles this year.
In terms of earnings, Rivian said it  …

title: Rivian downsizes new EV factory after Trump’s DOE slashes loan agreement
summary: Rivian has reduced its new Georgia facility to 300,000 units of annual capacity, down from the planned 400,000, following a DOE loan reduction from $6.6 billion to $4.5 billion under the Trump administration. Despite the scaled-back plans, the company expects to reach the lower capacity target sooner than originally scheduled.
content:
Rivian announced some changes today with regard to the factory its building in the state of Georgia. 
The company was planning to build the facility in two phases, each resulting in 200,000 vehicles of annual production capacity, for a total of 400,000 units. Rivian held a ground breaking ceremony late last year. 
Now the company says it is only planning for 300,000 units of annual capacity as a result of a revised loan agreement with the US Department of Energy - though its planning on hitting that annual capacity sooner than originally planned. DOE will now loan Rivian $4.5 billion, instead of the originally agreed amount of $6.6 billion …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: The logic of the racist Supreme Court isn’t adding up
summary: The Supreme Court's conservative majority struck down Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act in Louisiana v. Callais, enabling states like Louisiana to engage in racial gerrymandering without legal remedy. The decision represents a significant setback for voting rights protections established over decades of civil rights legislation.
content:
Close watchers of the Supreme Court knew that the conservative supermajority was about to murder what was left of the Voting Rights Act. Wednesday's decision in Louisiana v. Callais took down Section 2 of the law, clearing the way for racist gerrymandering, because it is now racist to remedy racism. The decision is an affront to the history of the Voting Rights Act, an affront to the history of the United States, and an affront to math. 
The state of Louisiana, which is around 30 percent Black, has six districts. The voting districts are drawn so that there are two majority-Black districts. That is two out of six districts; approximately 33 …
Read the full story at The Verge.

Microsoft is now rolling out its Xbox mode to all Windows 11 PCs. The new Xbox mode adds a full-screen interface to the Xbox PC app, much like Steam's Big Picture Mode, and originally debuted as the Xbox Full Screen Experience (FSE) with Asus' Xbox Ally devices.
"Some players in select markets will be able to download the Xbox mode experience today, with availability expanding to more players in those markets over the next several weeks," says the Xbox team. You'll be able to get the Xbox mode by installing the latest Windows update for Windows 11.
Xbox mode aims to try and bridge the gap between Xbox consoles and Windows, but its original …

title: Meta threatens to pull its apps from New Mexico if forced to make ‘technologically impractical’ changes
summary: Meta is threatening to withdraw Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp from New Mexico if required to implement regulatory changes the company deems technologically infeasible. The state attorney general is seeking sweeping changes including end-to-end encryption restrictions for minors and 99% detection of child sexual abuse material.
content:
Mark Zuckerberg. | Image: Cath Virginia / The Verge, Getty Images	

Meta says it may be forced to pull Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp from New Mexico if the attorney general gets his way. The state is demanding a host of changes that the company says are impossible to achieve. 
After winning a $375 million jury award against Meta in a trial that argued the company misled users in the state about the safety of its products, New Mexico Attorney General Raúl Torrez is asking the state court to order sweeping changes to the platforms. Among the asks are a prohibition on end-to-end encryption for minors, implementing age verification, and detecting 99 percent of new child sexual abuse material uploaded to its …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: With Saros, Housemarque makes a case for doing next-gen games differently
summary: Housemarque's new game Saros makes a case for prioritizing visual fidelity and aesthetic design as central to next-generation gaming experiences. The approach challenges conventional wisdom about substance over style in AAA game development.
content:
It is generally frowned upon to care too much about appearances. We have a lot of little aphorisms discouraging this - books and their covers, beauty being skin deep, style over substance, that sort of thing. Vanity is a risk. Should one put a disproportionate effort into how a thing looks, then said work may very well be considered shallow. But in the world of big-budget video games? That's how you win. 
Visual fidelity is video game shorthand for progress: how meticulously rendered a mountain is, how dynamically the snow behaves, how a player character raises their hands to touch a wall when the player approaches it just so. This pursuit  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: Elon Musk confirms xAI used OpenAI’s models to train Grok
summary: Elon Musk testified in court that his AI startup xAI used model distillation to train Grok on OpenAI's models, a practice where larger AI models transfer knowledge to smaller ones. The revelation highlights ongoing industry tensions over whether competitors are illegally copying larger models' capabilities.
content:
In a federal courtroom in California on Thursday, Elon Musk testified that his own AI startup, xAI, has used OpenAI's models to improve its own. 
The matter at question is model distillation, a common industry practice by which one larger AI model acts as a "teacher" of sorts to pass on knowledge to a smaller AI model, the "student." Although it's often used legitimately within companies using one of their own AI models to train another, it's also a practice that's sometimes used by smaller AI labs to try to get their models to mimic the performance of a larger competitor's model. 
Asked on the stand whether he knew what model distillation …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 埃隆·马斯克证实xAI使用OpenAI的模型来训练Grok
zh.summary: 埃隆·马斯克在法庭作证称，他的AI初创公司xAI使用了模型蒸馏技术在OpenAI的模型基础上训练Grok，即较大AI模型向较小模型转移知识的做法。这一披露突出了关于竞争对手是否非法复制较大模型能力的持续行业紧张关系。

title: Elon Musk testifies that xAI trained Grok on OpenAI models
summary: Elon Musk testified that xAI employed model distillation to train Grok using OpenAI's models, a technique frontier AI labs are working to prevent competitors from exploiting. This demonstrates the competitive pressures and IP concerns shaping AI development.
content: "Distillation" is a hot topic as frontier labs try to prevent smaller competitors from copying their models.

zh.title: 埃隆·马斯克作证xAI使用OpenAI模型训练Grok
zh.summary: 埃隆·马斯克作证，xAI采用模型蒸馏技术使用OpenAI的模型来训练Grok，这是前沿AI实验室正在努力阻止竞争对手利用的技术。这展示了塑造AI开发的竞争压力和知识产权问题。

title: FDA approval, fundraising, and the reality of building in healthcare according to BioticsAI founder
summary: BioticsAI CEO Robhy Bustami discussed how the company navigates FDA regulations and maintains team motivation while building in the highly regulated healthcare sector. The startup has successfully managed regulatory requirements alongside fundraising to advance its AI healthcare solutions.

title: Xbox owners can now disable Quick Resume for specific games
summary: Microsoft released an Xbox update allowing users to disable Quick Resume for individual games, addressing performance issues that sometimes occur with multiplayer titles and games requiring constant internet connections. The feature gives players greater control over the console's performance behavior.
content:
Microsoft has released a new Xbox update that adds a bunch of new features, including the ability to disable Quick Resume for individual games. 
Quick Resume, which lets you swap between your games with minimal wait time, is one of the best features on the Xbox Series X / S consoles. But it can also run into unexpected issues with multiplayer games or titles that require a constant internet connection. "We've heard your feedback around performance after long periods of inactivity or with games that rely heavily on online connections," Microsoft says. 

With Thursday's update, Microsoft also lets you make up to ten groups on their Home scree …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: Xbox用户现在可以为特定游戏禁用快速恢复功能

title: Beijing bans drone sales even as rest of world buys Chinese drones
summary: Beijing has implemented a citywide ban on drone sales, transport, and storage, even as the global market increasingly adopts Chinese-manufactured drones. The restriction represents a significant reversal of domestic drone adoption in China's capital.
content: Beijing's citywide ban restricts the sale, transport, and storage of drones.

zh.title: 北京禁止无人机销售，尽管全球其他地区正在购买中国无人机
zh.summary: 北京实施了城市范围内的无人机销售、运输和存储禁令，尽管全球市场越来越多地采用中国制造的无人机。这项限制代表了中国首都国内无人机采用的重大逆转。

title: Google’s Gemini AI assistant is hitting the road in millions of vehicles
summary: Google announced it will roll out its Gemini AI assistant to vehicles with Google built-in, replacing the current Google Assistant with a more advanced conversational AI. The upgrade marks a significant expansion of Google's push to enhance in-vehicle AI experiences.
content: Google announced on Thursday that it will begin rolling out Gemini to cars with Google built-in, marking a significant upgrade from the current Google Assistant. The move signals Google’s push to bring more advanced, conversational AI into the driving experience. The announcement follows closely behind news from General Motors, which revealed yesterday that Gemini is […]

zh.title: 谷歌Gemini AI助手即将在数百万辆车中上路
zh.summary: 谷歌宣布将在配备Google built-in的车辆中推出其Gemini AI助手，用更先进的对话式AI取代当前的Google助手。这次升级标志着谷歌在提升车载AI体验方面的一个重大扩展。

title: TikTok’s new ‘Campus Hub’ features college group chats and feeds
summary: TikTok launched Campus Hub with dedicated college group chats and personalized feeds designed to help students maintain campus community connections during breaks and transitions. The feature targets student users with community-focused tools.
content: The new hub features dedicated college group chats and personalized feeds designed to help students stay connected with their campus communities, even while they’re away for the summer.

title: Stripe introduces Link, a digital wallet that autonomous AI agents can use, too
summary: Stripe's Link digital wallet now enables autonomous AI agents to make purchases through approval flows, allowing secure authorization after connecting cards and subscriptions. The expansion reflects growing integration of AI capabilities into payment infrastructure.
content: Link lets users connect cards, banks, and subscriptions, then authorize AI agents to spend securely via approval flows.

zh.title: Stripe推出Link数字钱包，自主AI代理也可以使用
zh.summary: Stripe的Link数字钱包现在允许自主AI代理通过批准流程进行购买，在连接卡和订阅后允许安全授权。这一扩展反映了AI功能与支付基础设施的日益融合。

title: Meta is running get-rich-quick ads for its AI tools
summary: Meta's acquired company Manus is running controversial advertisements promoting easy money through AI services, including recruiting content creators to promote AI-powered website building. The campaign has drawn criticism for misleading marketing and undisclosed sponsorship relationships.
content:
Manus, an AI company Meta acquired for $2 billion last year is running ads promising quick, easy money with AI: Find local businesses without websites or with bad websites, have AI build them one, then call them up and sell it to them.
As part of the campaign, Manus was paying content creators to build out Instagram, YouTube, and TikTok accounts that promote its AI product as an easy, lucrative gig. (The creators' TikTok accounts were taken down after The Verge inquired about them.) Some of these videos would also appear as official ads for Manus, but the posts on the paid creator accounts themselves often obscured their ties to the company …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: Meta在为其AI工具投放快速致富广告

title: Cricut’s Joy 2 makes creating stickers easier for beginners, and it’s on sale
summary: The Cricut Joy 2 is an affordable, beginner-friendly cutting machine that enables users to create custom stickers, cards, and crafts using the Print Then Cut feature. The device is currently on sale with bundle options offering good value for hobbyists and creators.
content:
The Cricut Joy 2 doesn’t take up much desk space and looks pretty cute. | Image: Cricut	

If you’ve ever thought about making your own stickers for Etsy or just for fun but don’t know where to begin, I’d recommend taking a look at the Cricut Joy 2. I’ve been testing one for a couple of weeks now, and as someone completely new to the Cricut ecosystem, I’ve found it to be a relatively beginner-friendly way to get started. It also costs less than most Cricut models and is currently discounted as part of the company’s sale. While the machine itself is $99, the bundles offer better value right now: the Essentials Bundle starts at $119 ($20 off) at Amazon and Cricut and includes supplies for 35 projects, while the Ultimate Bundle is down to $169 ($50 off) at Amazon and Cricut, and adds extra tools — including an iron press — for over 75 projects right out of the box.
Cricut Joy 2

Where to Buy:

 $139 $119 at Amazon (Essentials bundle)
 $169 $209 at Amazon (Ultimate bundle)
 $139 $119 at Cricut (Essentials bundle)

Designed for smaller, everyday projects, the Cricut Joy 2 brings the company’s Print Then Cut feature to its entry-level lineup. That means you can print full-color designs using your home printer and then have the machine precisely cut them out, making it possible to create sticker sheets. In my testing, that’s been one of the most satisfying activities — and once you get the hang of it, it’s fairly quick and straightforward. (Just note that while you can use a laser printer, you’ll need to supply your own compatible sticker paper, as Cricut’s are designed for inkjet printers.) Beyond stickers, it can also create custom cards, simple vinyl decals, labels, bookmarks, and iron-ons, which I’ve found useful for both creative projects and more practical things like planner templates and drawer labels.
Setup is relatively straightforward, though Design Space — Cricut’s app for creating your design — does come with a bit of a learning curve. I struggled to get the ha

zh.title: Cricut的Joy 2让初学者更容易创建贴纸，目前在促销中
zh.summary: Cricut Joy 2是一台价格便宜、初学者友好的切割机，让用户能够使用"打印后切割"功能创建自定义贴纸、卡片和手工艺品。该设备目前正在促销中，提供多个捆绑选项，为爱好者和创意工作者提供良好的价值。

title: RFK Jr. appeals ruling that wiped out his vaccine advisory panel
summary: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is appealing a court ruling that dissolved his vaccine advisory panel, signaling his intent to continue fighting for its reinstatement. The appeal indicates Kennedy is pursuing legal remedies rather than alternative approaches.
content: There was uncertainty about whether Kennedy would fight or simply try an end run.

zh.title: 小肯尼迪对推翻其疫苗顾问小组的裁决提出上诉

title: Salesforce is crowdsourcing its AI roadmap — with customers
summary: Salesforce is allowing customers to directly influence its AI product roadmap, operating on the premise that challenges facing one enterprise customer typically affect others across the user base. The customer-driven approach aims to ensure AI priorities align with market needs.
content: Salesforce lets its customers lead its product roadmap with the thinking that if one enterprise customer has a problem, the others likely do too.

zh.title: Salesforce正在众包其AI路线图——与客户一起
zh.summary: Salesforce允许客户直接影响其AI产品路线图，基于以下前提：一个企业客户面临的挑战通常会影响整个用户群中的其他客户。这种客户驱动的方法旨在确保AI优先事项与市场需求保持一致。

title: Microsoft’s DLSS competitor is now available on the Xbox Ally X handheld
summary: Microsoft is testing its Automatic Super Resolution (Auto SR) feature on the Xbox Ally X handheld for docked play, improving resolution and frame rates when connected to larger screens. The technology, previously available on Copilot Plus PCs, addresses visual quality challenges on the portable device.
content:
Microsoft is starting to test its Automatic Super Resolution (Auto SR) feature with the Xbox Ally X. Auto SR first debuted on some Copilot Plus PCs nearly two years ago, improving visual quality and frame rates in select games. Now, Microsoft is testing it for docked play on the Xbox Ally X, allowing the 7-inch handheld to improve resolution and graphical settings for bigger TV screens.
"Docked play means larger screens and higher resolutions, where drops in image quality are more noticeable or where some games struggle to maintain smooth FPS," says Microsoft in a detailed blog post on Auto SR. "That's exactly the problem Auto SR was design …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 微软的DLSS竞争产品现已可在Xbox Ally X掌上设备上使用
zh.summary: 微软正在Xbox Ally X掌上设备上测试其自动超分辨率(Auto SR)功能,用于接驳到大屏幕时提高分辨率和帧率。这项技术之前在Copilot Plus PC上推出,解决了便携设备上的视觉质量问题。

title: Gemini is rolling out to cars with Google built-in
summary: Google is updating vehicles with Google built-in to include its Gemini AI assistant, replacing the older Google Assistant with improved conversational capabilities and vehicle-specific functions. The rollout will reach both new and existing vehicles through software updates.
content:
Here’s an early look at the new Gemini assistant on a vehicle infotainment system. | Image: Google	

Google is preparing to update vehicles that have Google built-in with its Gemini AI assistant.  This will be an upgrade from the current Google Assistant according to Google's announcement, and promises to provide an improved experience for natural conversations, fetching vehicle-specific information, settings adjustments, and more.
"When cars with Google built-in first hit the road in 2020, we made a commitment that your car will get better over time," Google senior product manager Alankar Agnihotri said in the announcement. "That means that Gemini is coming not only to new cars, but also to existing ones through a software update."
The a …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: Gemini正在推送至配备Google内置功能的汽车
zh.summary: 谷歌正在更新配备Google内置功能的车辆,添加其Gemini AI助手,以改进的对话能力和特定车辆功能取代旧的Google助手。推送将通过软件更新覆盖新车和现有车辆。

title: Here’s how the new Microsoft and OpenAI deal breaks down
summary: Microsoft and OpenAI have restructured their partnership in an amicable separation, allowing OpenAI to distribute products across all cloud providers instead of exclusively through Microsoft. The deal resolves long-standing tensions between the companies over AI infrastructure and business strategy.
content:
Microsoft's relationship with OpenAI has always been complicated, so I expected the close partnership-turned-situationship to end in tears. After all, executive disagreements, rearranged contracts, and frustrations over AI infrastructure have all regularly been part of the partnership, creating plenty of tension along the way. But against all odds, Microsoft and OpenAI divorced this week in a way that looks strangely amicable.
Microsoft announced the updates to its long-standing OpenAI deal on Monday, with the most important change allowing OpenAI to make its products and services available across all cloud providers. A day later, OpenAI an …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 微软和OpenAI新协议是如何分解的
zh.summary: 微软和OpenAI以友好分离的方式重组了合作伙伴关系,允许OpenAI在所有云提供商上分发产品,而不是仅通过微软独家分发。该协议解决了两家公司在AI基础设施和商业战略上长期存在的紧张关系。

title: Meta says its business AI now facilitates 10 million conversations a week
summary: Meta's AI tools for business are now facilitating 10 million conversations weekly, with over 8 billion advertisers having used at least one of its generative AI products. The scale demonstrates significant adoption of Meta's AI-powered business and advertising solutions.
content: Meta said over 8 billion advertisers have used at least one of its gen AI tools

zh.title: Meta表示其商业AI现在每周促进1000万次对话
zh.summary: Meta的商业AI工具现在每周促进1000万次对话，超过80亿广告主已经使用过其至少一款生成式AI产品。这种规模表明Meta的AI驱动商业和广告解决方案得到了广泛采用。

title: Instagram cracks down on content aggregators
summary: Instagram is cracking down on content aggregators that repost others' material without creating original content. The move targets low-effort accounts that simply re-upload other users' posts without attribution or original contribution.
content: The update targets content aggregators that don't post original content and instead simply re-upload others' posts.

zh.title: Instagram严厉打击内容聚合账户
zh.summary: Instagram正在严厉打击那些转发他人内容而不创建原创内容的内容聚合账户。此举针对那些不注明出处、不做原创贡献而仅仅重新上传其他用户帖子的低努力账户。

title: Instagram says it doesn’t want your tweet round ups
summary: Instagram is continuing its 2024 push to limit "unoriginal" content from reaching recommendation feeds and Explore tabs, including reposted Reels and repeatedly spammed clips. Facebook has adopted similar policies, where unoriginal accounts risk losing monetization capabilities.
content:
The internet is full of copycat, stolen, reposted, and low-effort content - and Meta, at least publicly, has said it is working to cut off some of the reach.
Beginning in 2024, the company has made incremental announcements saying it would begin limiting "unoriginal" content from being recommended on Instagram. It meant that if you were downloading and reposting someone's Reels, or spamming the same clip over and over, your content wouldn't show up in recommendation feeds or places like the Explore tab. Similar rules were later announced for Facebook, where "unoriginal" accounts risk losing their ability to monetize content. The idea is tha …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: Instagram表示不想要你的推文汇总
zh.summary: Instagram继续推进其2024年的措施，限制"非原创"内容出现在推荐源和探索标签中，包括转发Reels和反复推送的剪辑。Facebook也采取了类似政策，其中非原创账户可能面临失去变现能力的风险。

title: Is this ‘de-extinction’ project actually onto something?
summary: Colossal, a Dallas-based genetics startup backed by major venture capitalists and government interest, is developing projects to recreate extinct animals like the woolly mammoth and Tasmanian tiger through genetic engineering rather than DNA cloning. The company has raised hundreds of millions but faces questions about its methods and conservation impact.
content:
Dallas-based genetics and biotech startup Colossal has raised hundreds of millions of dollars from venture capitalists, the CIA, and Peter Thiel, among others. Its buzzy "de-extinction" projects aim to "bring back" lost animals like the woolly mammoth, the Tasmanian tiger, and the dire wolf - although it isn't creating copies of extinct creatures from ancient DNA, as the "de-extinction" tagline may suggest. In the case of the "dire wolves" presented to the world in 2025, the pups were gray wolves spliced with a few genetic traits to somewhat resemble the dire wolves. This ambitious branding opened the company up to questions about its conser …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 这个'物种复活'项目真的有前景吗?
zh.summary: 达拉斯生物技术初创公司Colossal获得主要风险投资和政府支持,正在开发通过基因工程而非DNA克隆技术来重新创造猛犸象和塔斯马尼亚虎等灭绝物种的项目。该公司已融资数亿美元,但其方法和保护影响面临质疑。

title: Meta lost 20 million users last quarter
summary: Meta reported losing 20 million "Family daily active people"—its combined user metric across Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, and Messenger—in the latest quarter. The company attributes the decline to internet disruptions in Iran and WhatsApp restrictions in Russia, while planning to increase AI investments by billions.
content:
Meta is planning to pump billions more into AI investments this year, despite noting that millions of users have seemingly started to abandon its platforms. In an earning call on Wednesday, Meta reported that figures for "Family daily active people" - the term Meta has coined for all collective users of Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, or Messenger - declined by 20 million this quarter compared to the previous three months.
Meta attributes this fall to "internet disruptions in Iran, as well as a restriction on access to WhatsApp in Russia." It's up to you whether you take Meta on its word, given that by bundling the user stats together across …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: Meta上季度流失2000万用户
zh.summary: Meta报告称在最近一个季度流失了2000万名'家庭日活用户'——这是其在Facebook、Instagram、WhatsApp和Messenger上的综合用户指标。该公司将用户流失归因于伊朗的互联网中断和俄罗斯对WhatsApp的限制,同时计划将AI投资增加数十亿美元。

title: OpenAI’s new security model is for ‘critical cyber defenders’ only
summary: OpenAI is launching GPT-5.5-Cyber, a specialized cybersecurity model restricted to vetted "cyber defenders" and institutions rather than the general public. CEO Sam Altman announced the limited rollout will begin within days to help organizations strengthen defenses against emerging threats.
content:
OpenAI is preparing to launch a new frontier cybersecurity model, GPT-5.5-Cyber. CEO Sam Altman said the model will not be available to the general public, but will be first rolled out to a select group of trusted "cyber defenders" in order for institutions to shore up their cyberdefenses. 
The limited rollout will take place "in the next few days," Altman said on X. "We will work with the entire ecosystem and the government to figure out trusted access for Cyber."
It's not clear who will get access to the model first, though previous "trusted access" schemes involved vetted professionals and institutions. Details of the model and its capa …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: OpenAI的新安全模型仅供'关键网络防御者'使用
zh.summary: OpenAI推出GPT-5.5-Cyber,一个专门的网络安全模型,仅限经过审查的'网络防御者'和机构使用。CEO山姆·奥特曼宣布该有限推出将在数天内启动,以帮助组织加强对新兴威胁的防御。

title: The more young people use AI, the more they hate it
summary: Gen Z is among the largest adopters of AI chatbots like ChatGPT, yet polling data shows young people are deeply skeptical and critical of AI technology. The trend contradicts tech industry narratives, with young people forming a significant part of the cultural backlash against AI.
content:
It's been almost three years since Silicon Valley started aggressively pushing large language model-based chatbots like ChatGPT as the supposedly inevitable future of everything, and there's no group that has felt the pressure quite like Gen Z.
Like with many tech trends before it, it's no surprise that young people are among the biggest adopters of AI chatbot tools. But contrary to the tales spun by tech companies like OpenAI and Google, polling data shows that Gen Z students and workers are a big part of the wider cultural backlash against AI. And even as they utilize these tools, vast swaths of young people are deeply acrimonious and eve …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 年轻人使用AI越多，就越讨厌它
zh.summary: Gen Z是ChatGPT等AI聊天机器人的最大采用者之一，但民调数据显示年轻人对AI技术深感怀疑和批评。这一趋势与科技行业的叙述相矛盾，年轻人形成了对AI文化反抗的重要组成部分。

title: DJI’s Osmo Pocket 4 is a better camera in every respect
summary: The DJI Osmo Pocket 4 is an evolutionary upgrade over the Pocket 3 with improvements throughout its camera sensor and controls, though it doesn't represent a major redesign. It is the first compact steadicam in this line to not launch in the US market following DJI's broader US distribution challenges.
content:
The Osmo Pocket 4 is no reinvention; it looks just like the Pocket 3.	

First they came for the drones, and now the vlogging cameras. DJI's Osmo Pocket 4 is the first of its compact steadicams not to launch in the US, following a string of DJI drones also missing the US market. 
The good news for American buyers is that the Pocket 4 is mostly an evolutionary upgrade, and there's little it does that the Pocket 3 doesn't (it looks like the rumored dual-lens Pro version will be more of a reinvention). The good news for everyone else is that those evolutions run throughout the Pocket 4, from the camera sensor to the controls. That makes this an unmistakable improvement over the previous generation.
DJI Osmo Pocket  …

Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 大疆Osmo Pocket 4在各方面都是更优秀的相机
zh.summary: DJI Osmo Pocket 4相比Pocket 3是一次进化升级，在相机传感器和控制方面均有改进，但不代表重大设计变更。由于大疆在美国的分销困难，这是该系列中首款未在美国市场推出的便携式稳定器。

title: PlayStation now requires a ‘one-time online check’ to confirm you own a game
summary: Sony clarified that PlayStation 5 and PS4 consoles require only a single one-time online check to verify game licenses, contradicting rumors about mandatory monthly re-validation. After the initial check, no further online verification is needed to play owned games.
content:
Sony has finally stepped in to clear up the growing confusion around a new DRM (Digital Rights Management) system on PlayStation 5 and PlayStation 4 consoles. Users reported that the system's latest updates seemingly introduced a requirement to go online once every 30 days to validate game licenses, but Sony now says this is false.
In a statement to GameSpot, a Sony Interactive Entertainment spokesperson said that "a one-time online check is required to confirm the game's license, after which no further check-ins are required." The DRM rumors had sparked concerns that gamers who don't sign in every month would lose access to their games by  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: PlayStation现在需要进行"一次性在线检查"以确认你拥有游戏
zh.summary: 索尼澄清PlayStation 5和PS4游戏机只需进行一次在线检查来验证游戏许可证，这与关于强制性月度重新验证的传言相矛盾。初始检查后，无需再进行在线验证就可以玩已拥有的游戏。

title: SoftBank is creating a robotics company that builds data centers — and already eyeing a $100B IPO
summary: SoftBank is launching a robotics company focused on building AI-powered data center infrastructure, with plans for a potential $100 billion IPO that underscores the intersection of robotics and AI infrastructure investment.
content: You need infrastructure to build AI a and robots, but apparently you also need AI and robots to build infrastructure.

zh.title: SoftBank正在创建一家建造数据中心的机器人公司——并已将目光投向1000亿美元IPO
zh.summary: SoftBank正在推出一家专注于构建AI驱动数据中心基础设施的机器人公司，计划进行可能达到1000亿美元的IPO，突显了机器人和AI基础设施投资的交汇点。

title: Amazon, Meta join fight to end Google Pay, PhonePe dominance in India
summary: Amazon and Meta are joining regulators to challenge Google Pay and PhonePe's 80% control of India's UPI payment network, lobbying for restrictions on the dominant digital payment platforms.
content: PhonePe and Google Pay command 80% of India's UPI instant payments network. Rivals are set to meet with regulators to lobby for restrictions.

zh.title: 亚马逊和Meta加入对抗Google Pay、PhonePe在印度主导地位的战争
zh.summary: 亚马逊和Meta正在加入监管机构，对Google Pay和PhonePe对印度UPI支付网络80%的控制权提出挑战，游说对主导性数字支付平台施加限制。

title: Amazon’s cloud business is surging — and so is its capital spending
summary: AWS revenue is exceeding expectations, but Amazon is committing to significant capital spending in the near term, according to CEO statements. This matters because it shows continued investment in cloud infrastructure growth.
content: The e-commerce giant is making more money than expected from AWS but it's also spending a lot, and will continue to do so in the near term, its chief executive said.

zh.title: 亚马逊的云业务飙升，资本支出也随之增加
zh.summary: AWS 收入超出预期，但根据首席执行官的声明，亚马逊承诺在短期内大幅增加资本支出。这很重要，因为它表明亚马逊继续投资云基础设施的增长。

title: Sources: Anthropic could raise a new $50B round at a valuation of $900B
summary: Anthropic has received multiple pre-emptive investment offers valuing the AI company between $850-900 billion. This reflects growing investor demand for AI capabilities as Anthropic positions Claude as a major competitor.
content: The maker of Claude has received multiple pre-emptive offers at valuations in the $850 billion to $900 billion range, according to sources familiar with the matter.

zh.title: 消息人士：Anthropic 可能以 9000 亿美元估值融资新一轮 500 亿美元
zh.summary: Anthropic 收到了多份主动投资提议，将这家人工智能公司估值在 8500 亿至 9000 亿美元之间。这反映了投资者对人工智能能力的需求不断增加，因为 Anthropic 将 Claude 定位为主要竞争对手。

title: Elon Musk’s worst enemy in court is Elon Musk
summary: During his second day of testimony in his case against OpenAI, Musk struggled with cross-examination, refusing direct answers and contradicting earlier statements, while jury members displayed visible skepticism. The performance undermined his legal position in attempting to dismantle OpenAI.
content:
Just a simple country CEO who was being tricked by that sophisticated lawyer | Image: Cath Virginia / The Verge, Getty Images	

About five hours into Elon Musk's testimony, I typed the following sentence into my notes: "I have never been more sympathetic to Sam Altman in my life."
Musk's direct testimony was an improvement over yesterday - even if his lawyer kept asking leading questions to cue him in how to answer. But that memory was immediately obliterated by an absolutely miserable cross-examination. For hours, Musk refused to answer yes or no questions with yes or no, occasionally "forgot" things he'd testified to in the morning, and scolded defense lawyer William Savitt. I watched a few jury members glance at each other. During one testy exchange, one woman wa …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 埃隆·马斯克在法庭上最大的敌人是他自己
zh.summary: 在与 OpenAI 案件的第二天证词中，马斯克在交叉询问中举步维艰，拒绝给出直接答案并与之前的陈述相矛盾，而陪审团成员表现出明显的怀疑。这次表现削弱了他试图瓦解 OpenAI 的法律立场。
